title: gagea villosa subsp. hervieri
intro: Gagea villosa subsp. hervieri, also known as Hervieri's marsh marigold, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the lily family (Liliaceae). The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its use in traditional medicine to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Gagea villosa subsp. hervieri include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of Gagea villosa subsp. hervieri can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of ailments, including respiratory infections and digestive issues.
